name: Belabartsaitsa Trail
name_original: Belabartsaitsa desde Isaba/Izaba – circular por el Valle de Belagua
description: Hike the moderate Belabartsaitsa circular trail from Isaba/Izaba, exploring diverse forests, a Romanesque bridge, and the Belabarce waterfal
region_intro: Hiking around Isaba/Izaba offers diverse landscapes within the Pyrenees, characterized by extensive Pyrenean plains and dense beech forests. The region features the unique karstic zone of Larra with its distinctive rock formations, alongside majestic mountain peaks such as Txamantxoia and Arlas. Valleys like Belagua, rivers, and waterfalls like Arrako Waterfall contribute to the varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Isaba/Izaba The most popular hiking route is Artikomendia Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route in the Roncal Valley features the Belabarzeko Urjausia waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Zemeto Local Trail, a moderate 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail winds through serene Pyrenean plains and beech forests. Local hikers also love the Belabartsaitsa Trail, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through varied Pyrenean landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Isaba/Izaba is defined by Pyrenean plains, beech forests, and significant mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountaineering routes. The routes in Isaba/Izaba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.35 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 3500 hikers have used komoot to explore Isaba/Izaba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Isaba/Izaba?
A: Isaba/Izaba offers a comprehensive network of over 60 hiking trails, catering to a wide range of fitness levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy walks to challenging mountaineering routes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Isaba/Izaba?
A: The region around Isaba/Izaba boasts incredibly diverse landscapes. You can explore extensive Pyrenean plains, dense beech forests, and the unique karstic zone of Larra with its distinctive rock formations. Majestic mountain peaks like Txamantxoia and Arlas offer panoramic views, while the beautiful Belagua Valley, rivers, and picturesque waterfalls add to the scenic charm.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls in the Isaba/Izaba area?
A: Yes, the Isaba/Izaba area features several beautiful waterfalls. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970293999/artikomendia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikomendia Trail&lt;/a&gt; includes the Belabarzeko Urjausia waterfall. Another option is the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966114897/arrako-waterfall-and-belagua-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrako Waterfall and Belagua Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the picturesque Arrako Waterfall. You can also discover more waterfalls in the region, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/876139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belabarzeko Urjausia - natural swimming pool&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4373323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrako Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Isaba/Izaba?
A: Yes, Isaba/Izaba offers several easy paths suitable for all fitness levels, including families. These trails are typically under two hours in duration, allowing for a relaxed outdoor experience. While specific family-focused routes aren't detailed, the variety of easy trails ensures options for those hiking with children.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Isaba/Izaba?
A: Yes, circular routes are available for hikers in Isaba/Izaba. A notable example for experienced trekkers is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966124914/txamantxoia-punta-maz-and-linza-refuge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txamantxoia (Punta Maz) and Linza Refuge Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive panoramic views. Another challenging circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966114897/arrako-waterfall-and-belagua-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrako Waterfall and Belagua Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Isaba/Izaba?
A: For experienced trekkers, Isaba/Izaba offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966124914/txamantxoia-punta-maz-and-linza-refuge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txamantxoia (Punta Maz) and Linza Refuge Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.6-mile route with substantial elevation gain, starting from the Refugio de Linza. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966114897/arrako-waterfall-and-belagua-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrako Waterfall and Belagua Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.5-mile trail encompassing the Rio Belagua.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Isaba/Izaba?
A: The hiking routes in Isaba/Izaba are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.35 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 3500 hikers have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from Pyrenean plains to dense beech forests and unique geological formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Isaba/Izaba?
A: Isaba/Izaba offers beautiful scenery throughout the year. While specific recommendations depend on your preferred activity and trail difficulty, the region's diverse flora, including alpine flowers, suggests spring and summer are ideal for vibrant landscapes. Autumn brings stunning colors to the beech forests. For challenging mountaineering, summer and early autumn are generally best, avoiding winter snows unless equipped for it.

Q: Are there any significant viewpoints or mountain peaks I can hike to?
A: Absolutely. The region is surrounded by significant peaks offering spectacular views. The ascent of Peak Arlas provides panoramic vistas of the Larra karstic zone and glimpses of the French lowlands from the Col de l'Arlas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966124914/txamantxoia-punta-maz-and-linza-refuge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txamantxoia (Punta Maz) and Linza Refuge Loop&lt;/a&gt; is also known for its expansive panoramic views of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking paths that pass through Isaba/Izaba?
A: Yes, Isaba/Izaba is a significant point along several renowned long-distance hiking and cycling paths. These include sections of the Euskal Herria footpath (GR 12), the Classic Swallows Route, and the Pyrenean Path (GR 11), offering opportunities for multi-day adventures.

Q: What are some easy hiking trails for beginners in Isaba/Izaba?
A: For beginners or those seeking a more relaxed walk, Isaba/Izaba offers several easy paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970745798/zemeto-local-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zemeto Local Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 2.7-mile path winding through serene Pyrenean plains and beech forests, making it a great option. There are 8 easy routes in total, providing accessible options for all.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical elements to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, some trails in the Isaba/Izaba region integrate cultural and historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970293999/artikomendia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikomendia Trail&lt;/a&gt; features historical Romanesque bridges. The region also has historical references to 'Contrabandistas' routes, hinting at its rich past and cross-border connections.
tips: Over the bed of the Belagua River is this old bridge from the Roman era with its characteristic arch shape, a true postcard. Next to it, a new bridge has been built since it has fallen into disuse.
From its 1,282 meters above sea level, the top of Belabarsaitsa offers wonderful views of the Pyrenees and the Belagua Valley. A landscape worthy of a postcard!

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Belabartsaitsa trail?
faqs.answer: The Belabartsaitsa trail is accessible from Isaba/Izaba. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a circular route starting from the village, parking would typically be available within or on the outskirts of Isaba/Izaba. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated hiker parking.

faqs.question: Is the Belabartsaitsa trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Belabartsaitsa trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through meadows where livestock might be present, or in areas with sensitive wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Belabartsaitsa trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but the best time to hike is typically from spring through autumn. In autumn, the diverse forests offer a particularly vibrant display of yellows, ochres, and greens. Summer allows for potential swimming at the Belabarce waterfall, while spring brings fresh greenery. Always check weather conditions before heading out, especially in the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Belabartsaitsa trail. It's a publicly accessible route within the natural landscape of the Belagua Valley.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Belabartsaitsa trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, immersing you in the characteristic landscape of the Belagua Valley. You'll traverse tranquil forests of beech, pine, and oak, cross open meadows, and follow paths alongside the river. While classified as moderate, the paths are generally easily accessible, though some sections might be uneven.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features several captivating points of interest. You'll encounter a charming Romanesque Bridge in the Roncal Valley, adding historical charm. A significant highlight is the stunning Belabarce waterfall, where you can even swim in its cold, clear waters during warmer months. Nearby is the mysterious Ibón cave, which can be explored with a flashlight, though access might be restricted during heavy rain. The route also leads to the Belabarsaitsa Summit, offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Is the Belabartsaitsa trail suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Yes, despite its 'moderate' classification, the Belabartsaitsa trail is often highlighted as a suitable option for families. Its manageable length and diverse natural features, including the waterfall and cave, make it an engaging outdoor activity for various fitness levels. Some sections may have noticeable inclines, but the paths are mostly accessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Belabarsaitsa, Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse, and E2. Errege bidea: Izaba - Belaguako aterpea, among others.

faqs.question: What should I know about exploring the Belabarce waterfall and Ibón cave?
faqs.answer: The Belabarce waterfall is a beautiful spot where you can swim in its cold, clear waters, especially in warmer months. For the mysterious Ibón cave, bring a flashlight if you wish to explore inside. Be aware that access to the cave might be restricted or difficult during periods of heavy rain when it fills with water.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts from Isaba/Izaba, you will find various amenities within the village. Isaba/Izaba offers cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation options, as well as tourist services. It's advisable to use facilities in the village before starting your hike, as there are no specific amenities directly on the trail itself.

waypoints.name: Romanesque Bridge in the Roncal Valley
